description_long: This is a two-story half-timbered house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles. The exterior walls feature a traditional timber frame pattern with infill panels, giving it a rustic, medieval European appearance. The house has symmetrical window arrangements on both floors, with white-framed windows and a central front door on the ground level. Two prominent stone chimneys rise from either side of the roof ridge, adding to the historic character. The structure sits on a stone or cobblestone base, surrounded by a patch of grass and stone pathway. The overall look suggests a well-preserved, old-world residential building suitable for a quaint urban or village setting.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, symmetrical windows, and dual stone chimneys, evoking a medieval European style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two windows on each floor, and the stone pathway leading to the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full height of the gable roof, symmetrical window placement, and both stone chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view with no visible door, featuring four windows arranged symmetrically and the roof's shingle texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with the same gable roof, window arrangement, and dual chimneys.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with small steps, flanked by windows, no porch or overhang, simple and functional.
